# targets

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `targets`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.directory` | `string` | Path to link apps relative to the home directory. |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable copying macOS applications to the user environment (works with Spotlight). |
| `targets.darwin.copyApps.enableChecks` | `boolean` | Whether to enable enable App Management checks. |
| `targets.darwin.currentHostDefaults` | `open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of anything` | Set macOS user defaults. Unlike {option}`targets.darwin.defaults`, the preferences will only be applied to the currently logged-in host. This distinction is important for networked accounts. Values set to `null` are ignored. ::: {.warning} Some settings might require a re-login to take effect. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.currentHostDefaults."com.apple.controlcenter".BatteryShowPercentage` | `null or boolean` | Whether to show battery percentage in the menu bar. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults` | `open submodule of attribute set of attribute set of anything` | Set macOS user defaults. Values set to `null` are ignored. ::: {.warning} Some settings might require a re-login to take effect. ::: ::: {.warning} Some settings are only read from {option}`targets.darwin.currentHostDefaults`. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari"."WebKitPreferences.developerExtrasEnabled"` | `null or boolean` | Configures the web inspector. ::: {.warning} Instead of setting this option directly, set {option}`IncludeDevelopMenu` instead. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".AutoFillCreditCardData` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable autofill of credit card numbers. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".AutoFillPasswords` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable autofill of usernames and passwords. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".AutoOpenSafeDownloads` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable opening of downloaded files. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".IncludeDevelopMenu` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable "Develop" menu in the menu bar. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".ShowOverlayStatusBar` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable status bar. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari".WebKitDeveloperExtrasEnabledPreferenceKey` | `null or boolean` | Configures the web inspector. ::: {.warning} Instead of setting this option directly, set {option}`IncludeDevelopMenu` instead. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.Safari.SandboxBroker".ShowDevelopMenu` | `null or boolean` | Show the "Develop" menu in Safari's menubar. ::: {.warning} Instead of setting this option directly, set {option}`"com.apple.Safari".IncludeDevelopMenu` instead. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.desktopservices".DSDontWriteNetworkStores` | `null or boolean` | Disable use of {file}`.DS_Store` files on network shares. See [the official article](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT208209) for more info. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.desktopservices".DSDontWriteUSBStores` | `null or boolean` | Disable use of {file}`.DS_Store` files on thumb drives. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.dock".autohide` | `null or boolean` | Hide the Dock automatically |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.dock".expose-group-apps` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable grouping of windows by application in Mission Control. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.dock".orientation` | `null or one of "left", "bottom", "right"` | Position of the Dock on the screen |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.dock".size-immutable` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable locking of the dock size. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.dock".tilesize` | `null or signed integer` | Sets the size of the dock. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.finder".AppleShowAllFiles` | `null or boolean` | Show hidden files in Finder |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.finder".FXRemoveOldTrashItems` | `null or boolean` | Automatically delete items from trash after 30 days |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.finder".ShowPathBar` | `null or boolean` | Show the path bar at the bottom of a Finder window |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.finder".ShowStatusBar` | `null or boolean` | Show the status bar at the bottom of a Finder window |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.battery".ShowPercent` | `null or one of "YES", "NO"` | This option no longer works on macOS 11 and later. Instead, use {option}`targets.darwin.currentHostDefaults.\"com.apple.controlcenter\".BatteryShowPercentage`. Whether to show battery percentage in the menu bar. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".IsAnalog` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable showing an analog clock instead of a digital one. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".Show24Hour` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable showing a 24-hour clock, instead of a 12-hour clock. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".ShowAMPM` | `null or boolean` | Show the AM/PM label. Useful if Show24Hour is false. Default is null. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".ShowDate` | `null or one of 0, 1, 2` | Show the full date. Default is null. 0 = When Space Allows 1 = Always 2 = Never |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".ShowDayOfMonth` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable showing the day of the month. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".ShowDayOfWeek` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable showing the day of the week. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.apple.menuextra.clock".ShowSeconds` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable showing the clock with second precision, instead of minutes. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.googlecode.iterm2".AddNewTabAtEndOfTabs` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable placement of new tabs at the end of the tab bar. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.googlecode.iterm2".AlternateMouseScroll` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable arrow keys when scrolling in alternate screen mode. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.googlecode.iterm2".CopySelection` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable copy to clipboard upon selecting text. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.googlecode.iterm2".ExperimentalKeyHandling` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable experimental key handling for AquaSKK compatibility. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults."com.googlecode.iterm2".OpenTmuxWindowsIn` | `null or signed integer` | Configures how to restore tmux windows when attaching to a session. **Possible Values** `0` : Native windows `1` : Native tabs in a new window `2` : Tabs in the attaching window |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleLanguages` | `null or (list of string)` | Sets the language to use in the preferred order. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleLocale` | `null or string` | Configures the user locale. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleMeasurementUnits` | `null or one of "Centimeters", "Inches"` | Sets the measurement unit. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleMetricUnits` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable the metric system. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.ApplePressAndHoldEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Repeat a key when it is held down (false) or display the accented character selector (true) |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleShowAllExtensions` | `null or boolean` | Always show file extensions in Finder |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.AppleTemperatureUnit` | `null or one of "Celsius", "Fahrenheit"` | Sets the temperature unit. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.KeyRepeat` | `null or signed integer` | Interval between key repetitions when holding down a key. Lower is faster. When setting through the control panel, 2 is the lowest value, and 120 the highest. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.NSAutomaticCapitalizationEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable automatic capitalization. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.NSAutomaticDashSubstitutionEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable smart dashes. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.NSAutomaticPeriodSubstitutionEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable period with double space. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.NSAutomaticQuoteSubstitutionEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable smart quotes. |
| `targets.darwin.defaults.NSGlobalDomain.NSAutomaticSpellingCorrectionEnabled` | `null or boolean` | Whether to enable spelling correction. |
| `targets.darwin.keybindings` | `attribute set of anything` | This will configure the default keybindings for text fields in macOS applications. See [Apple's documentation](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Cocoa/Conceptual/EventOverview/TextDefaultsBindings/TextDefaultsBindings.html) for more details. ::: {.warning} Existing keybinding configuration will be wiped when using this option. ::: |
| `targets.darwin.linkApps.directory` | `string` | Path to link apps relative to the home directory. |
| `targets.darwin.linkApps.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable linking macOS applications to the user environment. |
| `targets.darwin.search` | `null or one of "Bing", "DuckDuckGo", "Ecosia", "Google", "Yahoo"` | Default search engine. |
| `targets.genericLinux.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable settings that make Home Manager work better on GNU/Linux distributions other than NixOS. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable GPU driver integration for non-NixOS systems. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nixStateDirectory` | `absolute path` | The path to the Nix state directory. This only needs to be changed from default if the path was overridden, e.g., by setting the `NIX_STATE_DIR` environment variable. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable proprietary Nvidia drivers. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.sha256` | `null or string matching the pattern sha256-.*=` | The hash of the downloaded driver file. It can be obtained by running, for example, `sh nix store prefetch-file https://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86_64/@VERSION@/NVIDIA-Linux-x86_64-@VERSION@.run ` where `@VERSION@` is replaced with the exact driver version. If you are on ARM, replace Linux-x86_64 with Linux-aarch64. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.nvidia.version` | `null or string matching the pattern [0-9]{3}\.[0-9]{2,3}(\.[0-9]{2,3})?` | The exact version of Nvidia drivers to use. This version **must** match the version of the driver used by the host OS. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.packages` | `attribute set` | The Nixpkgs package set where drivers are taken from. |
| `targets.genericLinux.gpu.setupPackage` | `package` | Resulting setup package. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.defaultWrapper` | `one of "mesa", "mesaPrime", "nvidia", "nvidiaPrime"` | The package wrapper function available for use as `(config.lib.nixGL.wrap <package>)`. Intended to start programs on the main GPU. Wrapper functions can be found under `config.lib.nixGL.wrappers`. They can be used directly, however, setting this option provides a convenient shorthand. The following wrappers are available: - mesa - mesaPrime - nvidia - nvidiaPrime |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.installScripts` | `null or (list of (one of "mesa", "mesaPrime", "nvidia", "nvidiaPrime"))` | For each wrapper `wrp` named in the provided list, a wrapper script named `nixGLWrp` is installed into the environment. These scripts are useful for running programs not installed via Home Manager. The following wrappers are available: - mesa - mesaPrime - nvidia - nvidiaPrime |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.offloadWrapper` | `one of "mesa", "mesaPrime", "nvidia", "nvidiaPrime"` | The package wrapper function available for use as `(config.lib.nixGL.wrapOffload <package>)`. Intended to start programs on the secondary GPU. Wrapper functions can be found under `config.lib.nixGL.wrappers`. They can be used directly, however, setting this option provides a convenient shorthand. The following wrappers are available: - mesa - mesaPrime - nvidia - nvidiaPrime |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.packages` | `null or (attribute set)` | The nixGL package set containing GPU library wrappers. This can be used to provide OpenGL and Vulkan access to applications on non-NixOS systems by using `(config.lib.nixGL.wrap <package>)` for the default wrapper, or `(config.lib.nixGL.wrappers.<wrapper> <package>)` for any available wrapper. The wrapper functions are always available. If this option is empty (the default), they are a no-op. This is useful on NixOS where the wrappers are unnecessary. Note that using any Nvidia wrapper requires building the configuration with the `--impure` option. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.card` | `string` | Selects the non-default graphics card used for PRIME render offloading. The value can be: - a number, selecting the n-th non-default GPU; - a PCI bus id in the form `pci-XXX_YY_ZZ_U`; - a PCI id in the form `vendor_id:device_id` For more information, consult the Mesa documentation on the `DRI_PRIME` environment variable. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.installScript` | `null or one of "mesa", "nvidia"` | If this option is set, the wrapper script `prime-offload` is installed into the environment. It allows starting programs on the secondary GPU selected by the `nixGL.prime.card` option. This makes sense when the program is not already using one of nixGL PRIME wrappers, or for programs not installed from Nixpkgs. This option can be set to either "mesa" or "nvidia", making the script use one or the other graphics library. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.prime.nvidiaProvider` | `null or string` | If this option is set, it overrides the offload provider for Nvidia PRIME offloading. Consult the proprietary Nvidia driver documentation on the `__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD_PROVIDER` environment variable. |
| `targets.genericLinux.nixGL.vulkan.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Vulkan in nixGL wrappers. This is disabled by default because Vulkan brings in several libraries that can cause symbol version conflicts in wrapped programs. Your mileage may vary. |
